chapter_title: Furniture; bedding, mattresses, mattress supports, cushions and similar stuffed furnishings; luminaires and lighting fittings, not elsewhere specified or included; illuminated signs, illuminated name-plates and the like; prefabricated buildings

inclusions.description: Seats (whether or not convertible into beds) and parts thereof: chairs, sofas, armchairs, stools, benches, airline seats, vehicle seats (other than of heading 87.14), barber/dental chairs WITHOUT dental appliances

Medical, surgical, dental or veterinary furniture (e.g., operating tables, examination tables, hospital beds with mechanical fittings); barbers' chairs and similar chairs having rotating as well as both reclining and elevating movements; parts of the foregoing

Other furniture and parts thereof: desks, office furniture, kitchen furniture, bedroom furniture, shop furniture, laboratory benches, shelving units, wardrobes, bookcases, wall-mounted units, outdoor furniture

Mattress supports; articles of bedding and similar furnishing (e.g., mattresses, quilts, eiderdowns, cushions, pouffes and pillows) fitted with springs or stuffed or internally fitted with any material or of cellular rubber or plastics, whether or not covered (excluding pneumatic/water mattresses → Ch.39/40/63)

Luminaires (light fittings) and lighting fittings including searchlights and spotlights and parts thereof, not elsewhere specified or included; illuminated signs, illuminated name-plates and the like, having a permanently fixed light source, and parts thereof not elsewhere specified or included

Prefabricated buildings: buildings finished in the factory or put up as elements presented together to be assembled on site — including housing, worksite accommodation, offices, schools, shops, sheds, garages; also 'modular building units' of steel (container-sized, substantially/completely pre-fitted internally, designed to be assembled into permanent buildings) — Note 4

exclusions.condition: Pneumatic or water mattresses, pillows or cushions
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 39 (plastics), Chapter 40 (rubber) or Chapter 63 (textile made-up articles) depending on constituent material — Note 1(a)

exclusions.condition: Mirrors designed for placing on the floor or ground (cheval-glasses, swing-mirrors)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 70.09 (glass mirrors) — Note 1(b). Floor-standing mirrors are excluded from Ch.94 furniture despite their floor placement

exclusions.condition: Articles of Chapter 71 (jewellery, precious metal items, imitation jewellery)
Parts of general use (Note 2 to Section XV) of base metal, or similar goods of plastics, or safes of heading 83.03
exclusions.action: Redirect to Section XV (base metal), Chapter 39 (plastics), or heading 83.03 (safes) — Note 1(d). General-purpose screws, hinges, bolts used in furniture → Section XV

exclusions.condition: Furniture specially designed as parts of refrigerating/freezing equipment (84.18) or sewing machines (84.52)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 84.18 or 84.52 — Note 1(e). Furniture that is an integral part of a specific machine → classified with the machine

exclusions.condition: Lamps or light sources and parts thereof
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 85 — Note 1(f). Light SOURCES (bulbs, tubes, LEDs as components) → Ch.85. Complete LUMINAIRES (fittings that hold and direct light) → 94.05

exclusions.condition: Furniture specially designed as parts of audio equipment (85.18), audio/video recording apparatus (85.19 or 85.21), or broadcast/receiver apparatus (85.25–85.28)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 85.18, 85.22 or 85.29 respectively — Note 1(g). Speaker enclosures designed as part of a 85.18 apparatus → 85.18. TV cabinet integrated with the TV → 85.29

exclusions.condition: Articles of heading 87.14 (parts and accessories for vehicles of 87.09–87.13)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 87.14 — Note 1(h). Baby seats for bicycles, wheelchair accessories → 87.14

exclusions.condition: Dentists' chairs incorporating dental appliances of heading 90.18, or dentists' spittoons
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 90.18 (medical/dental instruments and appliances) — Note 1(ij). A dentist chair with integrated drill/suction unit is classified as dental apparatus, not furniture

exclusions.condition: Articles of Chapter 91 (clocks and clock cases)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 91 — Note 1(k). Grandfather clock cases → 91.12 (clock cases), not Ch.94 furniture

exclusions.condition: Toy furniture, toy luminaires and lighting fittings; billiard tables or other furniture specially constructed for games; furniture for conjuring tricks or decorations (other than lighting strings) such as Chinese lanterns
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 95.03 (toy furniture/luminaires), 95.04 (game furniture/billiard tables), or 95.05 (festive/conjuring articles) — Note 1(l)

exclusions.condition: Monopods, bipods, tripods and similar articles

classification_rules.rule: Note 2 — 'Furniture' (94.01–94.03) must be designed for floor/ground placement — with key exceptions
classification_rules.description: Articles of headings 94.01–94.03 are classified there ONLY IF designed for placing on the floor or ground. HOWEVER, the following are classified in 94.01–94.03 EVEN IF designed to be hung, wall-fixed or stacked: (a) cupboards, bookcases, shelved furniture (including single shelves with wall-fixing supports) and unit/modular furniture; (b) seats and beds. This creates two tiers: general furniture → must be floor-standing; wall units/seats/beds → can be wall-mounted/hung and still classified in Ch.94.

classification_rules.rule: Note 3(A) — Glass/marble tabletops and similar slabs are NOT parts of furniture
classification_rules.description: Parts of goods in headings 94.01–94.03 do NOT include sheets or slabs of glass (including mirrors), marble, other stone (Chapter 68) or ceramic (Chapter 69), whether or not cut to shape, UNLESS combined with other parts of furniture. A plain glass tabletop presented alone → Ch.70. A marble slab alone → Ch.68. Only when assembled/combined with a furniture frame or other furniture parts does the combined article go to Ch.94.

classification_rules.rule: Note 3(B) — Bedding articles of 94.04 presented separately are NOT parts of furniture (94.01–94.03)
classification_rules.description: Mattresses, mattress supports, cushions, pillows, quilts etc. of heading 94.04, when presented SEPARATELY (not as part of a complete furniture set), are NOT classified as 'parts' of seating (94.01) or other furniture (94.03). They stay in 94.04 as standalone articles. A separately presented cushion → 94.04. A cushion permanently attached to/integrated in a sofa → part of 94.01.

classification_rules.rule: Note 4 — Definition of 'prefabricated buildings' for heading 94.06
classification_rules.description: Prefabricated buildings (94.06) are: (a) buildings FINISHED IN THE FACTORY; OR (b) buildings put up as ELEMENTS presented together for on-site assembly — including houses, accommodation, offices, schools, shops, sheds, garages. Also includes MODULAR BUILDING UNITS of steel (container-sized/shaped, substantially or completely pre-fitted internally, designed to be assembled into permanent buildings). Key distinction from shipping containers (89.06): modular units are pre-fitted as habitable/usable spaces; empty containers for transport → 89.06.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 94.05 (luminaires — complete fittings) vs Ch.85 (lamps and light sources)
classification_rules.description: Ch.85 Note 1(f) excludes 'lamps or light sources and parts thereof' from Ch.94. The dividing line: Ch.85 = light SOURCES as components (bulbs, tubes, LED modules, drivers/ballasts); Ch.94 heading 94.05 = complete LUMINAIRES (the fitting/fixture that holds, directs and diffuses light, presented as a finished lighting unit). A pendant lamp complete with shade, socket and cord → 94.05. A replacement LED bulb → Ch.85. Chandelier with bulbs → 94.05.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 94.01 (seats) vs 94.02 (medical/barber chairs) vs 90.18 (dental chairs with appliances)
classification_rules.description: Three headings for chairs: 94.01 = ordinary seats including reclining seats, vehicle seats, adjustable office chairs. 94.02 = medical, surgical, dental or veterinary FURNITURE (operating tables, hospital beds, barber chairs with rotation+reclining+elevation); barber chairs that elevate, rotate AND recline → 94.02. 90.18 = dentist chairs when they INCORPORATE dental appliances (drill arm, suction unit, lamp arm) — the dental function dominates. A dental chair WITHOUT integrated dental equipment → 94.02.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 94.03 (furniture) vs other chapters for 'purpose-built' furniture
classification_rules.description: General furniture → 94.03. Furniture 'specially designed as part of' specific equipment is pulled into that equipment's chapter: refrigerator casework → 84.18; sewing machine cabinet → 84.52; speaker enclosure → 85.18; TV cabinet → 85.29. The test is whether the furniture is PURPOSE-BUILT as an integral component of a specific identified machine/equipment (→ machine chapter) or is general furniture that happens to be used near equipment (→ Ch.94).
